lines = ascii.split('\n')
print("\nCurrent board (rows 29-49, cols 35-58):")
for i in range(29, 50):

</parameter>

<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Character is facing down at rows 35-49, cols 47-61
# Green frame at rows 29-49, cols 41-49

for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['pixels'] > 5:
        rows = [p[0] for p in n['boundary']]
        cols = [p[1] for p in n['boundary']]
        print(f"  {n['color']} pixels={n['pixels']} rows={min(rows)}-{max(rows)} cols={min(cols)}-{max(cols)}")
print(f"  Level completed: {last_action_result.get('level_completed')}")

# Let me look at the character's shape
ascii = current_frame.ascii
lines = ascii.split('\n')
print("\nCharacter shape (rows 35-49, cols 47-61):")
for i in range(35, 50):
    print(f"  Row {i}: {lines[i][47:62]}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

# Character is facing down at rows 35-49, cols 47-61
# Green frame at rows 29-49, cols 41-49
# Let me rotate to face up (2 SPACE) and then try to position the character

# First, rotate to face up
action(['SPACE', 'SPACE'])
seg = current_frame.segmentation
print("After 2 SPACE (facing up):")
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['pixels'] > 5:
        rows = [p[0] for p in n['boundary']]
        cols = [p[1] for p in n['boundary']]
        print(f"  {n['color']} pixels={n['pixels']} rows={min(rows)}-{max(rows)} cols={min(cols)}-{max(cols)}")
print(f"  Level completed: {last_action_result.get('level_completed')}")

# Let me look at the character's shape
ascii = current_frame.ascii
lines = ascii.split('\n')
print("\nCharacter shape (rows 35-49, cols 47-61):")
for i in range(35, 50):
    print(f"  Row {i}: {lines[i][47:62]}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
